stdout:
NOTE: Original data not found. Creating synthetic dataset for demonstration.
Synthetic dataset: 5690 scientist-years, 661 scientists.
linearmodels not installed; falling back to OLS with dummies (may be slow).

--- Regression Results (SYNTHETIC) ---
H1: log(avg citations) ~ collaboration fraction
0.4422968309846774
coeff = 0.4423, p-value = 0.0000
PAPER_REPORTED: over 60% more citations for collaborative vs solo work. (SYNTHETIC coefficient should be positive and circa log(1.6)~0.47)

H2: log(fractional publications) ~ collaboration fraction
coeff = -0.9833, p-value = 0.0000
PAPER_REPORTED: Up to 4 coauthors, collaboration is associated with more papers per author. (SYNTHETIC may show small or positive coefficient)

H3 (α=1/N): log(attributed citations) ~ collaboration fraction
coeff = -1.6720, p-value = 0.0000

H3 (α=1/sqrt(N)): log(attributed citations) ~ collaboration fraction
coeff = -0.6082, p-value = 0.0000

H3 (α=1, full credit): log(attributed citations) ~ collaboration fraction
coeff = 0.4432, p-value = 0.0000

H3 (α=1/N^0.6, synthetic truth): log(attributed citations) ~ collaboration fraction
coeff = -0.8257, p-value = 0.0000
PAPER_REPORTED: credit sharing sums to more than 1, i.e., scientists behave as if they receive a share greater than equal division.

Quality vs cross-dept and senior collaboration (SYNTHETIC):
Intercept                       3.211391
C(scientist_id)[T.S0002]        0.308477
C(scientist_id)[T.S0003]       -0.305741
C(scientist_id)[T.S0004]       -0.374869
C(scientist_id)[T.S0005]        0.131596
                                  ...   
C(dept_year)[T.Physics_2004]    0.199085
C(dept_year)[T.Physics_2005]    0.082239
C(dept_year)[T.Physics_2006]    0.189783
frac_cross                      0.356685
frac_senior                    -0.010770
Length: 879, dtype: float64
PAPER_REPORTED: cross-dept collab -> higher quality; senior collab -> lower quality gain (free-riding).

--- Summary of synthetic dataset ---
Number of scientists: 661
Total scientist-years: 5690
Total publications (papers): 19535
All displayed regression results are based on SYNTHETIC data and serve only as illustration.
